title: phacelia lutea var. scopulina
intro: Phacelia lutea var. scopulina, also known as desert bluebird or yellow phacelia, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat coughs, colds, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Phacelia lutea var. scopulina is also known to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating bronchitis, and soothing digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its leaves and flowers can be dried and used in herbal preparations for respiratory support and as a natural remedy for various health ailments.
